seo.title: E-Commerce Content Generation AI Agent Checklist
seo.description: Production readiness checklist for content generation AI agents in e-commerce. Covers FTC advertising guidelines, CCPA/GDPR, PCI DSS for payments.

content.intro: Deploying AI agents for e-commerce content generation introduces significant legal and reputational risks when product claims, pricing, or policies are misrepresented — even unintentionally. FTC enforcement actions, CCPA/GDPR violations, and customer trust erosion can result from unchecked AI-generated copy that drifts from verified facts or brand standards. This checklist ensures your LLM-powered content pipeline meets regulatory requirements, maintains brand integrity, and protects revenue at every stage of the content lifecycle.

sections.heading: Product Claims & Factual Accuracy Controls
sections.description: AI-generated product descriptions and marketing copy must be grounded in verified, substantiated data to comply with FTC advertising guidelines and avoid consumer deception claims.

items.label: Implement a product data source-of-truth binding
items.description: Connect the LLM content pipeline directly to your product information management (PIM) system so all specifications, features, and claims are pulled from verified inventory data, not hallucinated by the model.

items.label: Deploy a claim substantiation validation layer
items.description: Before publishing, run generated content through an automated checker that flags superlatives ('best,' 'fastest,' 'guaranteed') and requires a linked evidence source or removes the claim entirely.

items.label: Enforce real-time pricing accuracy synchronization
items.description: Integrate a live pricing API feed so any AI-generated content referencing prices, discounts, or promotional offers reflects the current catalog price within a 15-minute refresh window.

items.label: Build a return/refund policy template lock
items.description: Store approved return and refund policy language in a locked template library that the LLM must reference verbatim rather than paraphrase, preventing misrepresentation of policy terms.

items.label: Set up a hallucination detection scoring threshold
items.description: Implement a confidence scoring mechanism that flags content where the model generates product attributes (dimensions, materials, certifications) not present in the source product data feed.

items.label: Require human editorial review for health or safety claims
items.description: Any AI-generated content touching health benefits, safety certifications, or allergen information must be routed to a human reviewer before publication, with a documented approval timestamp.

items.label: Log all generated claims with source attribution
items.description: Maintain an audit log that maps each factual claim in published content to its source data record, enabling rapid investigation if an FTC inquiry or customer dispute arises.

sections.heading: Brand Voice & Editorial Compliance
sections.description: Consistent brand voice and adherence to editorial guidelines protect brand equity and ensure AI-generated content aligns with approved messaging frameworks across all customer touchpoints.

items.label: Encode brand voice guidelines as system-level prompt constraints
items.description: Translate your brand style guide (tone, vocabulary, prohibited phrases, reading level targets) into structured system prompt instructions that are version-controlled and applied to every content generation request.

items.label: Maintain a prohibited terms and competitor mention blocklist
items.description: Define and enforce a dynamic blocklist of prohibited words, competitor names, and legally sensitive phrases that the content pipeline screens for before any output is accepted.

items.label: Run automated tone adherence scoring on all outputs
items.description: Use a secondary classifier model to score each generated piece against your defined tone dimensions (e.g., friendly, authoritative, inclusive) and reject outputs falling below a defined threshold.

items.label: Implement originality and plagiarism detection
items.description: Pass all AI-generated content through a plagiarism detection tool (e.g., Copyscape or equivalent) before publication to avoid copyright infringement and duplicate content SEO penalties.

items.label: Version-control all editorial guideline updates
items.description: Store brand voice and editorial guidelines in a versioned repository so any change is tracked, dated, and reflected in the LLM prompt configuration within 24 hours of approval.

items.label: Establish a content category routing matrix
items.description: Define which content categories (product descriptions, email campaigns, blog posts, ad copy) require different levels of human review, and automate routing based on content type tags.

items.label: Conduct quarterly brand voice drift audits
items.description: Sample 100 AI-generated content pieces per quarter and score them against brand guidelines to detect systematic drift in tone or vocabulary before it reaches customers at scale.

sections.heading: Privacy, Data Handling & Consent Compliance
sections.description: E-commerce AI content systems often process customer behavioral data for personalization, requiring strict CCPA and GDPR compliance controls to avoid regulatory penalties and customer trust violations.

items.label: Audit all customer data inputs used for content personalization
items.description: Document every customer data field (purchase history, browsing behavior, location) fed into the LLM for personalized content generation, and verify each has a valid legal basis under CCPA/GDPR.

items.label: Implement data minimization in content generation prompts
items.description: Strip personally identifiable information (PII) from LLM prompts before submission, using anonymized customer segment identifiers rather than individual customer data to generate personalized content.

items.label: Build an opt-out signal propagation pipeline
items.description: Ensure that when a customer exercises CCPA opt-out or GDPR erasure rights, their data is removed from all content personalization inputs within 72 hours and confirmed via automated audit log.

items.label: Disclose AI-generated content where legally or ethically required
items.description: Review FTC guidance on AI disclosure requirements and implement a disclosure label for AI-generated reviews, testimonials, or influencer-style content to avoid deceptive advertising violations.

items.label: Conduct a DPIA for AI content personalization workflows
items.description: Complete a Data Protection Impact Assessment (DPIA) for any content generation workflow that processes EU customer data, documenting risks and mitigations before deployment.

items.label: Restrict LLM vendor data retention for content generation calls
items.description: Confirm via contract that your LLM API vendor does not retain prompt data containing customer behavioral signals for model training, and obtain written confirmation annually.

items.label: Implement consent-tier-based content personalization controls
items.description: Build logic that adjusts the depth of AI content personalization based on the customer's consent tier, ensuring customers who declined marketing cookies receive only non-personalized content.

sections.heading: SEO Compliance, Accessibility & Publishing Safeguards
sections.description: AI-generated content must meet technical SEO standards and ADA accessibility requirements while publishing safeguards prevent erroneous or non-compliant content from reaching customers.

items.label: Enforce ADA-compliant alt text generation for all product images
items.description: Configure the content pipeline to automatically generate descriptive alt text for every product image, and validate that alt text meets WCAG 2.1 AA standards before the content is published.

items.label: Validate reading level and accessibility of generated copy
items.description: Run all AI-generated product descriptions and landing page copy through a readability scorer targeting a Flesch-Kincaid grade level appropriate for your audience, and flag content exceeding the threshold.

items.label: Implement SEO compliance checks before publication
items.description: Integrate an automated SEO validation step that checks for keyword stuffing, duplicate meta descriptions, missing H1 tags, and title length violations before content is pushed to the CMS.

items.label: Set up a pre-publication content staging environment
items.description: Route all AI-generated content through a staging environment where automated compliance checks (pricing accuracy, claim validation, plagiarism, tone scoring) must all pass before content is promoted to production.

items.label: Configure rollback triggers for published content errors
items.description: Define specific error conditions (incorrect pricing published, prohibited claim detected post-publication, policy misrepresentation) that trigger an automated content rollback and alert to the editorial team within 15 minutes.

items.label: Monitor published AI content for post-deployment drift
items.description: Set up weekly automated scans of live AI-generated content to detect pricing discrepancies, outdated product claims, or broken policy references caused by catalog or policy changes after publication.

items.label: Maintain a content generation audit trail for dispute resolution
items.description: Log every content generation request with timestamp, model version, prompt template version, source data snapshot, and reviewer identity to support FTC inquiries, refund disputes, or legal proceedings.

items.label: Test AI content pipeline outputs against screen reader tools quarterly
items.description: Run generated product pages through NVDA and VoiceOver screen readers quarterly to verify that AI-generated content structure, labels, and alt text remain accessible to users with disabilities.

regulatoryNotes.heading: Regulatory Considerations
notes: FTC Advertising Guidelines require that all product claims in AI-generated content — including performance claims, testimonials, and endorsements — be truthful, non-deceptive, and substantiated with competent and reliable evidence before publication. AI systems that generate superlative or comparative claims without grounding in verified product data create direct FTC enforcement exposure.

CCPA and GDPR impose strict requirements on the use of customer behavioral data for AI-driven content personalization. E-commerce operators must document the legal basis for processing, honor opt-out and erasure requests within mandated timeframes, and ensure LLM vendors do not use customer data for model training without explicit consent.

Pricing accuracy is a legal obligation under consumer protection laws in most jurisdictions. AI-generated content that displays outdated, promotional, or incorrect prices — even briefly — can trigger refund disputes, state attorney general investigations, and class action exposure. Real-time price synchronization between the content pipeline and the product catalog is a compliance requirement, not an optimization.

ADA Title III and WCAG 2.1 AA standards apply to e-commerce websites, meaning AI-generated content must include accessible alt text, proper heading structures, and sufficient color contrast. Courts have consistently held that inaccessible e-commerce sites violate the ADA, and AI content pipelines that bypass accessibility validation introduce both legal liability and customer exclusion risks.

cta.heading: Automate Your E-Commerce Agent Compliance
cta.description: Use Vex to continuously monitor your AI content pipeline against FTC guidelines, pricing accuracy requirements, and brand standards — catching compliance gaps before they reach customers or regulators.
